protectedRules: Never publish without a human-approved exact Live News article URL.
Never copy publisher wording, random comments, or private user information.
Never add facts that are missing from Live News story data, source metadata, or approved source research.
Never optimize for anger, panic, jealousy, or cheap engagement.
Use social patterns as structure only; never copy another creator, publisher, commenter, or account voice.
publicPatternPrinciples: Lead with one clear reason the story matters before adding the link.
Use a small number of relevant topic hashtags instead of broad tag stuffing.
Prefer exact article clicks, shares, saves, and clean discussion over likes alone.
Keep captions source-linked, factual, and human-readable before trying a stronger hook.
Treat public comments as aggregate confusion or interest signals only, never as source facts.

captionShapeMemory.useWhen: The headline is clear, factual, and strong enough to carry the post.
captionShapeMemory.avoidWhen: The title is long, vague, or needs context before it makes sense.

captionShapeMemory.useWhen: The summary contains the most useful human detail.
captionShapeMemory.avoidWhen: The summary only repeats the title or lacks safe detail.

captionShapeMemory.useWhen: Trust and attribution are the main reason to click.
captionShapeMemory.avoidWhen: The source line would make the caption feel stiff or robotic.

editorLessons: Live News social posts must send people to exact article pages, not only the homepage.
The system should learn from repeated aggregate patterns across posts, not one loud reaction.
Human approval remains part of the machine until quality is proven over time.
